def test_aabb_collisions(collision_system, boxes):
    # boxes is list of ((x1,y1,z1), (x2,y2,z2))
    results = collision_system.get_collision_pairs(boxes)
    
    # Sort results to make comparison easy
    sorted_results = sorted([tuple(sorted(pair)) for pair in results])
    
    # Expected pairs for this specific set
    # 0 and 1 overlap, 2 and 3 overlap
    expected = [(0, 1), (2, 3)]
    
    assert sorted_results == expected, f"Expected {expected}, but got {sorted_results}"
    print("Test passed!")

# Test data

    ((11, 11, 11), (13, 13, 13))
]

```python
import time

def test_performance(collision_system):
    # Generate 2000 non-overlapping boxes spread out
    # to see if the tree actually works or if it's just checking everything
    large_boxes = [((i*10, 0, 0), (i*10 + 1, 1, 1)) for i in range(2000)]
    
    start = time.time()
    collision_system.get_collision_pairs(large_boxes)
    end = time.time()
    
    duration = end - start
    print(f"Processing time: {duration:.4f}s")
    
    # We need this to be under 0.05s or the game frame rate will tank
    assert duration < 0.05, f"Too slow! Took {duration:.4f}s"
    print("Performance test passed!")

# Run the original test first, then the perf test
test_aabb_collisions(CollisionSystem(), test_boxes)
test_performance(CollisionSystem())
```
